label: Hari Seldon
description: The father of Psychohistory and founder of the two Foundations. Spent his life on Trantor predicting the future of human society.

label: Dors Venabili
description: A humaniform robot tasked by R. Daneel Olivaw to protect Hari Seldon. She became his wife and constant companion.

label: Yugo Amaryl
description: A heatsinker from Dahl who became Hari Seldon's greatest mathematical collaborator and the driving force behind the Prime Radiant.

label: Wanda Seldon
description: Hari Seldon's granddaughter and a powerful mentalic. She was instrumental in the creation of the Second Foundation.

label: Raych Seldon
description: Hari Seldon's adopted son from the Billibotton sector. An expert fighter and loyal protector of the Seldon family.

label: Cleon I
description: The Galactic Emperor during Hari Seldon's early career. Relied heavily on his First Minister, Eto Demerzel.

label: Eto Demerzel
description: An alias used by R. Daneel Olivaw to influence the Galactic Empire and protect Hari Seldon.

label: Linge Chen
description: The Chief Commissioner of Public Safety who effectively ruled the Empire and exiled Seldon to Terminus.

label: Salvor Hardin
description: The first Mayor of Terminus. Navigated the Foundation through its first two Seldon Crises without resorting to violence.

label: Hober Mallow
description: The first Merchant Prince. He used economics and trade rather than religion to expand the Foundation's power.

label: Poly Verisof
description: High Priest of the Foundation's scientism religion and ambassador to Anacreon.

label: Lord Dorwin
description: An imperial diplomat who visited Terminus, known for speaking without actually saying anything of substance.

label: Wienis
description: The power-hungry Prince Regent of Anacreon who attempted to conquer the Foundation.

label: Bel Riose
description: A brilliant Imperial General who tried to conquer the Foundation but was stopped by the dead hand of Hari Seldon's psychohistory.

label: Ducem Barr
description: A Siwennian patrician who secretly aided the Foundation against Bel Riose.

label: Cleon II
description: The last strong Galactic Emperor, whose paranoia led him to recall Bel Riose.

label: Brodrig
description: Cleon II's trusted, corrupt secretary who accompanied Bel Riose and was subsequently executed.

label: The Mule
description: A mutant warlord who disrupted the Seldon Plan by conquering the Foundation with his emotional control abilities.

label: Bayta Darell
description: The woman who deduced The Mule's identity and killed Ebling Mis to protect the location of the Second Foundation.

label: Toran Darell
description: Husband to Bayta Darell, accompanied her in the flight from the Mule.

label: Ebling Mis
description: The greatest psychologist of the First Foundation, who discovered the location of the Second Foundation but was killed before he could reveal it.

label: Han Pritcher
description: An intelligence agent for the Foundation who was later converted into an unwavering loyalist by The Mule.

label: Bail Channis
description: A Second Foundation agent who tricked The Mule into believing the Second Foundation was on Tazenda.

label: Arcadia Darell
description: Bayta Darell's granddaughter who unwittingly carried a message to the First Foundation that the Second Foundation was 'at the other end of the Galaxy'.

label: Dr. Darell
description: Invented the Mentalic Static device that allowed the First Foundation to capture Second Foundationers.

label: Pelleas Anthor
description: A supposed First Foundation researcher who was secretly a Second Foundation agent.

label: Homir Munn
description: A collector of Mule memorabilia who went to Kalgan to investigate The Mule's origins.

label: Preem Palver
description: The First Speaker of the Second Foundation who orchestrated a masterful deception to put the Seldon Plan back on track.

label: Golan Trevize
description: A Foundation councilman with an unerring ability to make the right decision. He chose the fate of the Galaxy (Galaxia).

label: Janov Pelorat
description: A mythologist obsessed with finding the origin planet, Earth.

label: Bliss
description: A young Gaian woman who travels with Trevize and Pelorat, representing the collective consciousness of Gaia.

label: Fallom
description: An intersex Solarian child taken aboard by Trevize and later merged with R. Daneel Olivaw.

label: Sura Novi
description: An agent of Gaia posing as a simple peasant woman to observe the First Foundation.

label: Stor Gendibal
description: An ambitious Speaker of the Second Foundation who raced to find the mysterious anti-Mule force.

label: Harla Branno
description: The strong-willed Mayor of Terminus who tried to assert the First Foundation's dominance through sheer military and technological power.

label: R. Daneel Olivaw
description: A humaniform robot spanning the entire history of the Galaxy. Guided humanity via the Zeroth Law.

label: Elijah Baley
description: A Plainclothesman who solved interstellar murders, overcame his agoraphobia, and inspired humanity to settle the stars.

label: Gladia Delmarre
description: A Solarian woman who moved to Aurora, became Baley's lover, and later a symbol of spacer-settler cooperation.

label: Han Fastolfe
description: The creator of R. Daneel Olivaw and the leading voice on Aurora for allowing Earthmen to colonize the Galaxy.

label: Kelden Amadiro
description: Fastolfe's rival, who wanted Spacers to rule the Galaxy and actively tried to destroy Earth.

label: Vasilia Aliena
description: Fastolfe's estranged daughter, who unwittingly unlocked R. Giskard's telepathic abilities.

label: R. Giskard Reventlov
description: A telepathic robot who formulated the Zeroth Law of Robotics to protect humanity as a whole.

label: R. Jander Panell
description: A humaniform robot practically identical to Daneel. His 'murder' caused a major crisis on Aurora.

label: Julius Enderby
description: Baley's boss in the NYPD, who was secretly involved in a spacer murder conspiracy.

label: R. Sammy
description: A simple robot assigned to the NYPD. Was destroyed to hide evidence.

label: Trantor
description: An ecumenopolis (city-planet) covering its entire landmass, serving as the capital of the Galactic Empire.

label: Terminus
description: A remote planet on the edge of the galaxy, home to the First Foundation.

label: Earth
description: The radioactive birthplace of humanity, slowly forgotten by the rest of the galaxy.

label: Aurora
description: The oldest and most powerful of the Spacer worlds, heavily reliant on robotic labor.

label: Solaria
description: A sparsely populated Spacer world where humans live in extreme isolation, communicating only via holograms.

label: Gaia
description: A planet where all matter—living and inanimate—shares a single collective consciousness.

label: Kalgan
description: An aristocratic resort world that briefly became the capital of the Mule's Union of Worlds.

label: Smyrno
description: One of the Four Kingdoms bordering Terminus. Birthplace of Hober Mallow.

label: Anacreon
description: The most aggressive of the Four Kingdoms that broke away from the Galactic Empire.

label: Haven
description: A rugged planet of independent traders, home to Toran Darell.

label: Sayshell
description: An independent planetary sector that managed to remain neutral during the Mule's conquests.

label: Comporellon
description: A cold, puritanical planet, originally known as Baleyworld, settled by Earthers.

label: Synnax
description: A water-world on the periphery of the Empire, birthplace of Gaal Dornick.

label: Siwenna
description: A former capital of the Normannic Sector, heavily devastated by Imperial forces.

label: Tazenda
description: A cold planet mistaken by the Mule to be the home of the Second Foundation.

label: Rossem
description: A barren, icy world used as a political prison, controlled by Tazenda.

label: Psychohistory
description: The mathematical science predicting the behavior of vast populations, relying on the ignorance of the subjects.

label: Prime Radiant
description: A device storing the complete equations of psychohistory, accessible only by the Second Foundation.

label: Visi-Sonor
description: A musical instrument that directly stimulates the visual cortex. Played masterfully by Magnifico (The Mule).

label: Positronic Brain
description: The sponge-like platinum-iridium brain that gives robots consciousness and enforces the Three Laws.

label: Three Laws of Robotics
description: The core programming of positronic brains: 1. Do not harm humans. 2. Obey orders. 3. Protect own existence.

label: Zeroth Law
description: A law formulated by Giskard and Daneel: A robot may not harm humanity, or, by inaction, allow humanity to come to harm.

label: Mentalic Static Device
description: An electronic device creating severe mental pain to telepaths, protecting the wearer from mind control.

label: Neuronic Whip
description: A common hand weapon that stimulates the pain nerves directly, capable of stunning or killing.

label: Encyclopedia Galactica
description: The compendium of all human knowledge, compiled by the First Foundation on Terminus.

label: Galactic Empire
description: The galaxy-spanning human empire that ruled for 12,000 years before slowly decaying and falling.

label: First Foundation
description: The technological powerhouse established on Terminus to shorten the galaxy's dark age from 30,000 years to 1,000.

label: Second Foundation
description: A secret society of mentalics hiding at 'Star's End', tasked with maintaining the Seldon Plan.

label: Spacers
description: The first wave of human colonists. Long-lived, reliant on robots, and intensely agoraphobic and germaphobic.

label: Settlers
description: The second wave of colonists from Earth. Short-lived but dynamic, rejecting robot labor.

label: Julius Enderby
description: Commissioner of the New York City Police Department and secretly an anti-Spacer fanatic.

label: R. Sammy
description: A clumsy administrative robot at the NYPD, murdered to cover up a conspiracy.

label: Dr. Sarton
description: An Auroran roboticist who designed R. Daneel Olivaw, murdered in Spacetown on Earth.

label: Jessie Baley
description: Elijah Baley's wife, briefly involved in a Medievalist conspiracy on Earth.

label: Hannibal Leebig
description: A Solarian roboticist who tried to bypass the First Law of Robotics by redefining what a 'human' is to a robot.

label: Jothan Leebig
description: A Solarian who suffered from severe agoraphobia.

label: Rikaine Delmarre
description: Gladia's husband on Solaria, murdered due to his political and genetic ideologies.

label: Gremionis
description: An Auroran citizen who repeatedly offered Gladia 'viewing' and 'seeing' privileges.

label: Mandamus
description: An Auroran scientist working with Amadiro who designed the Nuclear Intensifier to irradiate Earth.

label: D.G. Baley
description: A descendant of Elijah Baley who helped Gladia and Giskard stop Amadiro.

label: Nuclear Intensifier
description: A device designed to increase the natural radioactivity of a planet's crust. Used on Earth.

label: Baleyworld
description: The first Settler world, named after Elijah Baley, later known as Comporellon.

label: Joseph Schwartz
description: A 20th-century tailor randomly transported thousands of years into the future to a radioactive Earth.

label: Bel Arvardan
description: An Imperial archaeologist who theorized Earth was the birthplace of humanity.

label: Lord Ennius
description: The Imperial Procurator of Earth during the attempted rebellion.

label: Biron Farrill
description: Son of the Rancher of Widemos, forced to investigate a conspiracy against the Tyranni.

label: Aratap
description: A highly intelligent and pragmatic Tyranni commissioner.

label: Rik
description: A spatio-analyst whose mind was wiped after discovering Florina's sun was about to go nova.

label: Valona March
description: A Florinian peasant who took care of the amnesiac Rik.

label: Florina
description: The only planet capable of growing the immensely valuable kyrt.

label: Sark
description: The planetary neighbor that brutally oppressed Florina to control the kyrt trade.

label: Tyrann
description: A harsh, militaristic planet that conquered dozens of neighboring star systems in the Nebular Kingdoms.

label: Lewis Pirenne
description: Chairman of the Board of Trustees of the Encyclopedia Galactica, lacking political vision.

label: Anselm haut Rodric
description: A crude nobleman from Anacreon sent to negotiate with Terminus.

label: Jorane Sutt
description: Secretary to the Mayor, who tried to use religion to control the Foundation but was outmaneuvered by Mallow.

label: Jaim Twer
description: A Foundation trader who was secretly an agent for Jorane Sutt.

label: Sennett Forell
description: The illegitimate son of Hober Mallow and a wealthy Merchant Prince during the war with Bel Riose.

label: Lathan Devers
description: A Foundation trader who tried to bribe Brodrig and ultimately fled to Trantor to stop Bel Riose.

label: Onum Barr
description: An impoverished Siwennian patrician who helped Hober Mallow find the truth about Imperial nuclear power.

label: Magnifico Giganticus
description: The Mule's disguise as a pitiful clown, traveling with Bayta and Toran Darell.

label: Lady Callia
description: The seemingly foolish mistress of Lord Stettin, who was secretly a Second Foundation agent.

label: Lord Stettin
description: The ruler of Kalgan who launched a disastrous war against the First Foundation.

label: Jole Turbor
description: A holovision journalist who participated in the conspiracy to find the Second Foundation.

label: Laskin Joranum
description: A charismatic populist who claimed to be from Mycogen but was actually from a fringe world, attempting to overthrow Demerzel.

label: Chetter Hummin
description: Another alias of R. Daneel Olivaw, posing as a journalist to aid Hari Seldon.

label: Mandell Gruber
description: A simple Imperial gardener who panicked at being promoted and assassinated Cleon I.

label: Clowzia
description: A woman who briefly worked with Hari Seldon in the Dahl sector.

label: Mycogen
description: An isolationist, hyper-traditionalist sector of Trantor, populated by descendants of Spacer worlds (specifically Aurora).

label: Dahl
description: A lower-class, geothermal-powered sector of Trantor, home to Yugo Amaryl and Raych.

label: Wye
description: A wealthy sector of Trantor situated at the South Pole, controlling much of the planet's heat-sink infrastructure.

label: Rashelle
description: The ambitious Mayor of the Wye sector who attempted to secede from the Empire.

label: Hiroko
description: A Comporellian Minister of Transportation who interacted with Golan Trevize.

label: Bander
description: A hermaphroditic Solarian who attempted to kill Trevize, Pelorat, and Bliss but was killed by Bliss's mentalic powers.

label: Galaxia
description: The ultimate endpoint of the Seldon/Daneel plan: a living galaxy where every atom is part of a collective consciousness.

label: Alpha
description: A terraformed planet orbiting Alpha Centauri, settled by the last refugees of Earth's radioactive crust.

label: Monolee
description: An Alphan who encountered Trevize and explained the history of New Earth.

label: The Far Star
description: A highly advanced gravitic spaceship gifted to Golan Trevize for his mission.

label: Jump Drive
description: The technology that allows faster-than-light travel by 'jumping' through hyperspace.

label: Blaster
description: A standard energy weapon used by military and civilians throughout the galaxy.

label: Personal Shield
description: A Foundation invention that wraps the wearer in an invisible force field, absorbing kinetic and energy attacks.

label: Isaac Asimov
description: The grandmaster of science fiction who created the Foundation, Robot, and Empire universes.

label: Settler Ship 1
description: A ship carrying Settlers to new worlds.

label: Settler Ship 2
description: A ship carrying Settlers to new worlds.

label: Settler Ship 3
description: A ship carrying Settlers to new worlds.

label: Settler Ship 4
description: A ship carrying Settlers to new worlds.

label: Settler Ship 5
description: A ship carrying Settlers to new worlds.

label: Settler Ship 6
description: A ship carrying Settlers to new worlds.

label: Settler Ship 7
description: A ship carrying Settlers to new worlds.

label: Settler Ship 8
description: A ship carrying Settlers to new worlds.

label: Settler Ship 9
description: A ship carrying Settlers to new worlds.

label: Settler Ship 10
description: A ship carrying Settlers to new worlds.

label: Spacer World 1
description: One of the original 50 Spacer worlds.

label: Spacer World 2
description: One of the original 50 Spacer worlds.

label: Spacer World 3
description: One of the original 50 Spacer worlds.

label: Spacer World 4
description: One of the original 50 Spacer worlds.

label: Spacer World 5
description: One of the original 50 Spacer worlds.

label: Spacer World 6
description: One of the original 50 Spacer worlds.

label: Spacer World 7
description: One of the original 50 Spacer worlds.

label: Spacer World 8
description: One of the original 50 Spacer worlds.

label: Spacer World 9
description: One of the original 50 Spacer worlds.

label: Spacer World 10
description: One of the original 50 Spacer worlds.

label: Foundation Mayor 1
description: A historical Mayor of Terminus.

label: Foundation Mayor 2
description: A historical Mayor of Terminus.

label: Foundation Mayor 3
description: A historical Mayor of Terminus.

label: Foundation Mayor 4
description: A historical Mayor of Terminus.

label: Foundation Mayor 5
description: A historical Mayor of Terminus.

description: A historical Mayor of Terminus.

label: Foundation Mayor 7
description: A historical Mayor of Terminus.

description: A historical Mayor of Terminus.

description: A historical Mayor of Terminus.

description: A historical Mayor of Terminus.
